programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1996, a group of political extremists robbed a bank in Washington using homemade bombs and military precision to escape. The FBI learned that the robbers might be preparing for a small-scale war with law enforcement.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The FBI discovered that the criminals had a hidden agenda beyond their personal wealth.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Boston, FBI agents tracked down a gang of robbers who specialized in armored car takedowns. As the robberies grew increasingly more violent, authorities launched a complex sting to trap the deadly ring of thieves.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1996, a series of bank robberies in Texas baffled authorities. But when similar thefts occurred the next year in California, FBI agents linked the crimes and established a modus operandi.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Angered by the government's policy in the Middle East, Mir Qazi brutally gunned down two members of the CIA as they waited in their cars at a stoplight. The killer fled the country and was placed on the FBI Ten Most Wanted Fugitives list.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In July 1997, three people were shot to death at a Georgetown coffee shop. Investigators could find no immediate suspect or no clear motive for the murders. Was this a hate crime, a robbery gone wrong or an act of cold-blooded vengeance?

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Arizona, a shipment of money vanished without a trace, along with the two men hired to guard it. Agents didn't know if the guards were willing participants or victims of an ambush. Suspicions grew that the crime was an inside job.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a rash of home invasions plagued the Detroit area. Each time, masked men disguised as police officers broke in with assault rifles and stole money and jewelry. But when one homeowner shot back, the identity of the intruders was revealed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 2000, the FBI was overwhelmed with investigations in Seattle which had the third-highest number of bank robberies in the nation. Agents focused on a series of heists in which two heavily armed men seemed to know where the banks stored their money.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A Milwaukee teen was kidnapped by four gunmen who demanded $30,000 from his family for his safe return.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Chicago police and the FBI began to suspect that a law enforcement officer was responsible for the crimes.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In August 1992, a man returned to his Memphis home and discovered that his wife was missing. The husband, his friends, family and neighbors began to receive ransom calls.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, Jamie McMahan and Christopher Kauffman went on a crime spree of robbery, kidnapping and murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the 1980s, the FBI investigated wide-scale corruption among the detectives of the San Juan, Puerto Rico Police Department. Drug trafficking, extortion, kidnapping and murder-for-hire were among the department's known illicit activities.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1999, a brutal serial killer who traveled by train terrorized Texas. The FBI assembled a special task force to profile the violent drifter and help locate him.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: The story of Tyler Davis Bingham, pretty criminal turned leader of the brutal and feared Aryan Brotherhood.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In January 1973, 20-year-old mother of one Dawn Magyar vanished while on a shopping trip in Owosso, Michigan.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Jordan Monaghan killed his 24-day-old daughter Ruby on New Year's Day in January 2013. In August of that year, when his partner tried to end their relationship, he killed his son Logan, who was 21 months old.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the cold-blooded serial killer who took the lives of eleven people during a terrifying decade-long murder campaign.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Dublin man Eric Locke refused to accept it when Sonia Blount, a mother of one, ended their relationship. After resorting to begging, stalking, threats and manipulation, he created a fake social media profile, posing as a potential love interest.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Paul Michael Stephani has become etched in the minds of all who knew him as the notorious weepy voiced Killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman's death in Pennsylvania triggers a homicide investigation into another woman's death in North Carolina.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A car carrying three men pulls up alongside another on an Alaskan highway and fires shots, leaving a passenger dead. One of the passengers in the killer's car agrees to testify against his friends. The resulting trials don't end the carnage.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police discovered the body of Douglas Beamish's estranged wife, authorities linked 20 cat hairs found on her jacket to a cat owned by Beamish's parents. It was the first time animal DNA was used to solve a criminal case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Genell Plude is found dead in her bathroom, the scene points to suicide. But a coroner's inquest and a unique application of forensic science gave investigators a different explanation for her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappeared along a California highway in 1991. Three years later, the case was solved when a man's refrigerated truck was looked upon with suspicion.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Soon after becoming pregnant by Dr. Maynard Muntzing, firefighter Michelle Baker's health quickly deteriorates. Eventually, she raises suspicions that Muntzing is doing her harm.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man died in his home after a history of heart disease. Rumors surfaced that the death was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 1996 investigation into 48-year-old Martha Hansen's murder is helped by a legal requirement that bars have video cameras installed on the premises.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On the way to Yosemite National Park, a car carrying three Secret Service agents collided with another car.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2002, the eyes of the world were on Virginia and Maryland. A serial sniper was on the loose, killing innocent, unsuspecting citizens. Just how was he caught?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother of two young children was found dead in her bedroom. Her death was ruled a suicide - but when investigators learned she had almost died in a house fire three years earlier, they decided to take another look at the evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: No one in a residential community saw anything the day one of their neighbors was brutally murdered. Fingerprints found at the crime scene helped investigators to apprehend the presumed killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Firefighters found an entire family dead inside their home it looked like a murder-suicide, but there were several inconsistent clues in the rubble.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, California firefighters battled ten arson fires in three weeks. When cigarettes and a scrap of paper connected the case to similar fires further north, the hunt was on for a dangerous pyromaniac.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Janet Overton died suspiciously in 1988, and the investigation hit a dead end when the autopsy indicated no foul play was involved. A telephone tip and the sensitive nose of a forensic examiner indicated otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man riding a bicycle was fatally injured, and police believed he was the victim of a hit-and-run accident. Tiny clues found at the scene created a picture of the vehicle which struck him and led police to its driver.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A computer crash pushed a manufacturing company to the brink of collapse. Investigators must determine if the crash was the result of a sabotage.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A police officer accused of killing his estranged wife insists she committed suicide. Investigators say it was murder - that it was physically impossible for the woman to have shot herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Colorado hunter is the victim of a well-planned crime. Investigators suspect two weapons and two killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A coroner finds telltale clues on the badly burned body of a murder victim, sending police in pursuit of a couple of killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A California teen went missing. Police suspected she'd run away until her body was discovered in a ditch.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a serial killer was terrorizing the city of St. Louis, the FBI didn't need lab tests, fingerprints or any of the usual methods. All they needed was a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a talented television news anchor is shot to death outside her home, it appears to be a crime of passion perpetrated by an obsessed fan.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A go-go dancer told her family she needed to get away for a while and then she simply disappeared. Investigators were suspicious, because she took neither her car nor her clothes. 20 years later, her family received a call that changed everything.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An Alaskan police officer finds a woman's body while on routine patrol of a public park. During the autopsy, the medical examiner preserves the portion of her rib cage which sustained the fatal wound.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After the murder of Seattle residents, evidence from the couple's dog eventually ties a murderer to the scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial arsonist starts several fires that cause millions of dollars worth of damage and the deaths of three people. He is discovered, partly through the use of forensic hypnosis.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Haunted by the disappearance of her mother, a young woman undertakes an investigation of her own. In her mother's diary, she discovers a dark family secret that might explain the disappearance.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A nurse experiences a variety of flu-like symptoms. None of her doctors are able to discover the cause, until she visits the gynecologist for a routine check-up.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When two women from the same town were murdered in the same way, police feared a serial killer was on the loose. At first they thought the victims had nothing in common until they found tiny clues linking them to the same man.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When hunters found a skull in a Texas canyon, police found bits of clothing, small bones and a strand of hair. A forensic artist reconstructed her face. Now all they had to do was find her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy real estate tycoon goes missing, it appears to be foul play. In his will, he leaves instructions in case he should die under violent circumstances - which were carried out after a hiker comes across his bullet-ridden skull.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man who had committed crimes including murder was not caught. Almost 50 years later, advances in technology and handwriting analysis uncovered the criminal.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of 16-year-old Leanne Tiernan was discovered nine months after she disappeared. Forensic scientists found clues that painted a virtual portrait of her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a man is shot down in his garage, police discover that a wound on the widow may have been self-inflicted.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young TV news producer is raped and murdered in her apartment. Investigators turn to the Commonwealth of Virginia's DNA Databank.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of assault with a bat and pepper spray. Illicit drug use is reported to be going on in a feed store parking lot.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A bear is reported to be wandering near people's homes. Deputies respond to a domestic violence call.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of a man and woman fighting. Deputies investigate a reported verbal disturbance outside an apartment complex.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Two people are discovered sleeping in a car in a high school parking lot. A deputy patrolling Lake Tahoe finds two women who fell out of their kayaks.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of a dispute and finds a house destroyed by an intoxicated person.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A woman with felony warrants is staying at a known drug house. The occupants of a vehicle in a routine traffic stop claim to be sovereign citizens and that the law doesn't apply to them. A burglary at a business complex.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy investigates a report of a man strangling a woman. Deputies respond to a report of a person screaming obscenities and throwing chairs. A deputy responds to a call for assistance from a department store loss prevention officer.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A pizza shop owner tells the police that there is a crazed, naked man running around in his kitchen. A deputy is called to a home where suspected parental abuse and child abuse is being reported. Verbal disturbance over a Jeep.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: The investigation into a case of domestic abuse leads to the arrest of the abuser's friend on a $25,000 warrant. A deputy investigates two women who are parked behind a closed business late at night.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Detectives and deputies arrest sex traffickers in a prostitution sting operation. A store owner reports a suspicious man looking into car windows. A deputy is called to a business where four people sitting in a car appear to be up to no good.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A troubled young man with physical limitations decides to fight with the police. The boat patrol on Lake Tahoe responds to an overturned vessel in the water. A vehicle with an out-of-date registration is pulled over.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies struggle with a wanted man who had too much to drink. A deputy deals with two young thieves. A young man bleeding from the head gives an officer a difficult time.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: A Day at the Drug House
programme.desc: A deputy follows two probationers into a known drug house. A water company worker contacting people about a water issue calls police about an extremely inebriated woman who fell down.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: A Big Dog & Bunches of Meth
programme.desc: Police respond to a scene where a person at gunpoint is in a vehicle reported as stolen. A man reportedly jumps from a moving car. Drugs are found in a vehicle that is stuck in a puddle.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An illegal marijuana grow operation is found in a mobile home. A deputy responds to a report of a loud and violent neighbor. A home burglary is interrupted by the homeowners.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a 9-1-1 call about a possible burglary. Deputies answer a domestic violence call, where a man claims his wife choked him.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies respond by boat to a report of an injured woman. A 92-year-old man with dementia becomes lost.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies investigate a report of a woman throwing objects at passing cars. According to a domestic violence call, an intoxicated woman throws heavy objects at her husband.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of domestic abuse along with an assault with a deadly weapon. A traffic stop on a vehicle with an expired registration reveals three women who have some surprising drug habits.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies respond to a report of an inebriated man trying to change a tire on the side of the road. Police investigate a report of a suspicious person in the back of a store loading items onto his truck.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Four Punks with Guns Making Nightmares That Are Real
programme.desc: Deputies search for four armed men who broke into homes then assaulted and robbed citizens. A deputy checks on the welfare of a woman who had recently given birth.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Follow the Bike with No Light
programme.desc: A bicycle stopped for not having lights leads to the discovery of a stolen 27-foot trailer and its parolee occupants. Police respond to a brawl in a motel parking lot.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A man with multiple arrests for heroin possession talks about what life on the drug is really like.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Responding to a domestic violence call, a deputy confronts an angry, non-compliant man who refuses to identify himself. Deputies try to find out who shot a family's dog. A man is passed out in a truck.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: We follow Deputy Bryan Payne up close and personal on his night shift as he goes about a typical day for a deputy in El Dorado County.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a fight at a popular bar. A person is screaming in a mall parking lot. Two people are caught breaking into an apartment.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An anonymous caller gives the whereabouts of a stolen vehicle. The driver of a car stopped at an intersection is unresponsive. A deputy responds to a report of a vehicle that may have been used in an earlier crime.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police visit a known drug house in Lake Tahoe. A convicted felon has a weapon. A burglar tries to escape in a stolen vehicle.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies respond to a homeowner's report that people were stealing items from a neighbor's house. A woman is found driving her car with no license plate and a flat tire. A deputy is flagged down by another motorist to report an accident.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies respond to a parolee who is locked in a residence and known to have a weapon. A trained dog finds clues that a human would surely miss.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies respond to a vehicle fire on a highway. A man claims local citizens have been stealing from his store. Officers assist at Lake Tahoe's SnowGlobe Music Festival. A young woman with a substance abuse problem escapes custody.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An intoxicated young man exposes the dysfunctional mind of an alcoholic. A known drug user is found roaming a department store parking lot.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies wishing to be part of the SWAT team face a testing procedure that is harsh and demanding.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responding to a call in the outlying area of the county finds a large illegal marijuana grow operation. An officer investigates several reports of petty theft.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Officers do a probation search of a known heroin user. A person with two felony warrants has a propensity to evade capture. A motorcyclist is pinned between vehicles after an accident.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An attempted suicide is reported. An officer responds to an animal abuse call. Police do a gang sweep of a residential area in Lake Tahoe.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Officers respond to an ongoing fight between drug users. A deputy responds to a report of a drug overdose. A woman complains she is being harassed because she is of Mexican descent.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy is called to assist after a hit-and-run accident. A 9-1-1 caller reports a fight between a husband and wife. Deputies respond to a report from a mother that her vehicle was taken without permission by her daughter.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Smile, You're on My Surveillance System!
programme.desc: The Special Enforcement Detail prepares to take down a person with a felony warrant for theft and drug possession. Deputies investigating a disturbance in a field discover minors in possession of drugs and alcohol.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police execute a search for heroin in a horrifically filthy house. A report of suspicious activity in a grocery store parking lot. Officers investigate an abandoned trailer.
